commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch treats Readability output equal to the page title as failed extraction, falls back to body-first basic HTML cleanup, and adds a regression test for that shape.

PR surface: Source +41, Tests +28. Total +69 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the minimized failure shape: current main accepts any non-empty Readability text, and the PR test models a title-only Readability result with body HTML. The original reporter's exact URL or HTML is still not available.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blockers are concrete repair items: preserve the provider fallback order and avoid changing the public SDK helper behavior.

target.title: fix #82685: [Bug]: web_fetch fails to extract full HTML body content
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #82685.

- Treat Readability output that only repeats the page title as a failed body extraction and fall back to basic HTML cleanup.
- Make basic HTML cleanup prefer visible `<body>` content when a document body exists, while keeping the page title as metadata.
- Add regression coverage for the title-only Readability result shape.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `web_fetch` no longer returns head/title text instead of body text when the HTML body has visible content and the Readability path does not produce useful body content.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux Node runtime in the issue worktree, calling the actual `createWebFetchTool` path against a live HTTPS HTML response from `httpbingo.org` with proxy-backed network access enabled.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `http_proxy=http://proxyhk.zte.com.cn:80 https_proxy=http://proxyhk.zte.com.cn:80 all_proxy= node --import tsx/esm --input-type=module <live web_fetch proof script>`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the real `web_fetch` tool call:

```json
{
  "status": 200,
  "contentType": "text/html",
  "extractor": "raw-html",
  "textIncludesBody": true,
  "textIncludesTitleBodyPrefix": false
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The live command returned a successful HTML fetch through the real tool path, selected the `raw-html` extractor, included the visible body marker, and did not return a title/body prefix.
- **What was not tested:** The original reporter did not provide their exact URL or HTML file, so the live proof uses a minimized HTML response with a visible body marker and the regression coverage pins the title-only Readability branch directly.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/web-readability/web-content-extractor.test.ts src/agents/tools/web-tools.readability.test.ts src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** `web_fetch` accepted any non-empty Readability result as final content, even when the result only repeated the document title and did not represent body extraction. The basic HTML cleanup also rendered the whole document first, so head/title text could be mixed into fallback output instead of treating `<body>` as the primary content source.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The runtime now recognizes title-only Readability output as an unusable body extraction and re-runs basic HTML cleanup before returning. That cleanup now renders `<body>` content first when present, so the fallback path returns visible body text while preserving the title separately as metadata.

## Patch quality notes

- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** Medium-high; the issue lacks the reporter's exact HTML, but the change is limited to the HTML extraction decision point and has both live tool-path proof and regression coverage.
- **Why this is not a mitigation:** The patch changes the extraction invariant rather than adding a provider-specific fallback: title-only readable output is no longer considered successful body extraction, and basic cleanup now uses the document body as its source of truth.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
